html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 漏三下 - An ancient timekeeping method using a water clock, indicating around 3 AM.
  • 绾堕马髻 - A traditional Chinese hairstyle where the hair is piled up on the top of the head, resembling a horse falling over.
  • 衣红绡之衣 - Wearing a red silk robe, symbolizing luxury and beauty.
  • 灯花影中，欢笑弥畅 - A poetic description of the dim light of a lamp, often used to create a romantic or intimate atmosphere.
  • 水舌挢不能下 - A metaphor for being speechless, often used to describe a state of awe or amazement.
  • 雪压层檐重，风欺半臂单 - A poetic description of the harsh conditions of early winter.
  • 联句 - A collaborative form of poetry, often used in social gatherings to entertain and engage participants.
  • 阁笔而起 - To put down the pen and stand up, often used to describe the end of writing or composing.
  • 数曰不入巢园 - Several days without entering the garden, often used to describe a period of absence or neglect.
  • 阴廊之间，渐有苔色 - A shaded corridor gradually covered with moss, often used to describe a neglected or overgrown area.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 道光癸卯闰秋 - The leap month of the autumn season in 1823 during the Daoguang era of the Qing dynasty.
  • 秋芙 - Likely a nickname or pet name for a woman, possibly the author's wife or a close female relative.
  • 堕马髻 - A traditional Chinese hairstyle where the hair is piled up on the top of the head, resembling a horse falling over.
  • 红绡之衣 - A red silk robe, symbolizing luxury and beauty.
  • 灯花影中 - A poetic description of the dim light of a lamp, often used to create a romantic or intimate atmosphere.
  • 小年嬉戏之事 - Childhood memories and playful activities, often cherished and nostalgic.
  • 诗词 - Poetry and verse, a significant part of Chinese literature and culture.
  • 水舌挢不能下 - A metaphor for being speechless, often used to describe a state of awe or amazement.
  • 初冬诗 - Poetry written during the early winter season.
  • 雪压层檐重，风欺半臂单 - A poetic description of the harsh conditions of early winter.
  • 阿翘假托 - A nickname, possibly referring to a specific person or a literary character.
  • 桂帐虫飞 - A luxurious tent or canopy, often associated with wealth and comfort.
  • 素馨 - A fragrant flower, often used in poetry to describe beauty and fragrance.
  • 联句 - A collaborative form of poetry, often used in social gatherings to entertain and engage participants.
  • 归宁 - A term used to describe a woman returning home to visit her parents, often after marriage.
  • 群季 - Younger brothers, often mentioned in family contexts.
  • 青绫 - A type of fine silk, often used in clothing and bedding to indicate luxury.
  • 夜深有人，尚徘徊风露下 - A poetic description of someone lingering late at night, often used to convey a sense of longing or nostalgia.

Proper Nouns

  • 道光 - The era name of the Daoguang Emperor of the Qing dynasty.
  • 癸卯 - The stem-branch year name for 1823.
  • 闰秋 - The leap month of the autumn season.
  • 秋芙 - A person's name, likely a nickname or pet name.
  • 漏 - An ancient water clock.
  • 堕马髻 - A traditional Chinese hairstyle.
  • 红绡 - A type of red silk.
  • 灯花 - The wick of a lamp.
  • 诗词 - Poetry and verse.
  • 水舌挢 - A metaphor for being speechless.
  • 初冬诗 - Poetry written during the early winter season.
  • 雪压层檐重，风欺半臂单 - A specific line of poetry.
  • 阿翘 - A nickname, possibly referring to a specific person or a literary character.
  • 桂帐 - A luxurious tent or canopy.
  • 素馨 - A fragrant flower.
  • 联句 - A collaborative form of poetry.
  • 归宁 - A term used to describe a woman returning home to visit her parents.
  • 群季 - Younger brothers.
  • 青绫 - A type of fine silk.
  • 夜深有人，尚徘徊风露下 - A poetic description of someone lingering late at night.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 半出余授 - Partially taught by me, indicating the author's contribution to the lady's musical education.
  • 因病废辍 - Interrupted due to illness, describing a period of inactivity caused by health issues.
  • 指法渐疏 - Fingering technique gradually became less precise, indicating a decline in skill due to lack of practice.
  • 强为理习 - Forced to practice, suggesting an effort to regain lost skills despite difficulties.
  • 调弦既久，高不成音 - After tuning for a long time, it could not produce a high note, indicating a problem with the instrument.
  • 再调则当五徽而绝 - Upon retuning, it broke at the fifth position, indicating a specific point of failure.
  • 索上新弦 - Asked for a new string to be put on, describing the action taken to fix the instrument.
  • 烟雾迷空，窗纸欲黑 - Smoke filled the air, making the window paper almost black, describing a fire incident.
  • 不戒 - Careless or negligent, indicating the cause of the fire.
  • 火延幔帷 - The fire spread to the curtains, describing the extent of the damage.
  • 扑之始灭 - It was extinguished after being fought, describing the effort to put out the fire.
  • 其谶不远 - The omen was not far off, suggesting a prophetic interpretation of the broken string.
  • 况五，火数也 - Especially the number five, which is associated with fire, indicating a numerological or symbolic connection.
  • 应徽而绝 - Broke at the position, suggesting a precise point of failure.
  • 琴其语我乎 - Is the lute speaking to me? indicating a metaphorical or symbolic interpretation of the event.
  • 金盆捣戎葵叶汁 - Pounded the juice of the *Rongkui* leaves in a golden basin, describing a specific method of preparation.
  • 杂于云母之粉 - Mixed with talcum powder, describing the mixture used.
  • 用纸拖染 - Applied with a brush, describing the method of application.
  • 虽澄心之制，无以过之 - Even if it were made with utmost care, it would not surpass this, indicating the quality of the result.
  • 诗成不用苔笺写，笑索兰闺手细钞 - Poems do not need to be written on moss paper; instead, they are happily copied by the hands of a refined lady, describing the method of copying the poem.
  • 秀媚可人 - Graceful and charming, describing the quality of the writing.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 秋芙 - The name of the lady being described, likely a nickname or a reference to a historical figure.
  • 夕阳红半楼 - Refers to a specific location, possibly a tower or a building with a view of the setting sun.
  • 雏鬟 - Refers to young maidservants, indicating the household setting.
  • 魏滋伯，吴黟山两丈之门 - Refers to the doors of two respected scholars, indicating the lady's education and cultural background.
  • 晋唐格 - Refers to the style of calligraphy from the Jin and Tang dynasties, indicating the lady's aspirations in calligraphy.

Proper Nouns

  • 秋芙 - The name of the lady being described.
  • 夕阳红半楼 - A specific location, possibly a tower or a building.
  • 魏滋伯 - A respected scholar.
  • 吴黟山 - Another respected scholar.
  • 郭季虎 - A person who took away a document.
  • 《秋林著书图》 - The title of a painting.
  • 《西湖百咏》 - The title of a document.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 夏夜苦熱 - A descriptive phrase indicating extreme heat.
  • 約游 - Classical way of saying 'made an appointment to tour' or 'arranged to visit.'
  • 甫 - Means 'as soon as.'
  • 殷殷 - Onomatopoeic expression for deep, rolling thunder.
  • 強趣 - Means 'to urge or force forward despite resistance.'
  • 永及 - Classical expression meaning 'reached' or 'arrived at.'
  • 瞑合 - Means 'to merge into darkness' or 'to become dim and indistinct.'
  • 雨下如注 - Vivid idiom for heavy rain.
  • 雨霁 - Means 'clear after rain.'
  • 騷騷 - Onomatopoeic expression for the sound of wind through bamboo.
  • 萬翠濃滴 - Poetic way of describing lush greenery.
  • 殘妝美人 - Classical metaphor comparing natural scenery to a woman’s appearance.
  • 秀色可餐 - Idiom meaning 'so beautiful it is almost edible.'
  • 衣袂 - Refers to the sleeves of one’s robe.
  • 開士 - Term for a Buddhist monk or teacher.
  • 伊蒲 - Term for a vegetarian dish, likely *iru* or *iru* rice, common in Buddhist monasteries.
  • 畫帧 - Framed painting.
  • 空到色香何有相 - Buddhist-style philosophical question about the nature of form and emptiness.
  • 若離文字豈能禪 - Philosophical statement on the relationship between language and spiritual understanding.
  • 茶話 - Formal or informal discussion over tea.
  • 塢 - Means a small village or settlement near a hill.
  • 亂石排拱 - Poetic description of natural rock formations.
  • 幾察 - May be a misreading or variant meaning 'almost like' or 'seemingly.'
  • 磐磴 - Refers to stone steps or platforms.
  • 《平沙落雁》 - Classical zither composition.
  • 滃然 - Describes clouds that are dense or thick.
  • 涧水互答 - Poetic description of a natural sound.
  • 幾忘我兩人猶生塵世間也 - Buddhist metaphor for spiritual transcendence.
  • 殘暑漸收 - Poetic way of saying the summer was ending.
  • 暝煙 - Dusk mist.
  • 里許 - Means 'about a mile.'
  • 蘇堤 - Famous causeway in Hangzhou.
  • 是曰 - Classical way of referring to a specific day.
  • 屋漏 - The roof leaked.
  • 鎖扃 - Locked and bolted.
  • 蝶帳蚊橱 - Mosquito net and mosquito cabinet, referring to bed furnishings.
  • 半為澤國 - Metaphor for being soaked by water.
  • 熨之 - Poetic way of saying 'to press or steam dry.'
  • 五鼓 - Fifth drum strike at night, marking the early hours of the morning.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 理安寺 - Buddhist monastery, likely located in Hangzhou.
  • 月查開士 - Buddhist monk or teacher who presided over the monastery.
  • 白蓮畫帧 - Painting of a white lotus, a common Buddhist symbol of purity and enlightenment.
  • 《平沙落雁》 - Classical zither composition, often associated with the Tang dynasty.
  • 石屋洞 - Natural cave or grotto near a mountain area.
  • 楊梅塢 - Small settlement near a hill or mountain.
  • 蘇堤 - Famous causeway in Hangzhou, constructed during the Song dynasty by Su Shi.
  • 蝶帳蚊橱 - Traditional Chinese bed furnishings designed to keep out insects.
  • 筠籠 - Bamboo steamer or basket used for drying or pressing clothes.
  • 五鼓 - Fifth drum strike at night, a traditional timekeeping system in ancient China.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 喜繪牡丹 - Loves to paint peonies. '喜' (likes/loves) and '繪' (paint) are common in classical contexts.
  • 下筆頗自矜重 - She was quite self-conscious when she put pen to paper. '自矜重' implies a sense of self-importance or self-restraint in her artistic process.
  • 活色生香 - Classical idiom meaning 'vivid and fragrant,' used to describe something that is lively and evocative, often in literary or artistic contexts.
  • 品葉評花 - Appraising leaves and flowers, a metaphor for discussing or evaluating art, poetry, or aesthetics.
  • 不減當年 - No less than in her prime. A common expression used to describe something that retains its former quality or vigor.
  • 不勝賓朋零落之感 - Could not help but feel the sorrow of friends' dispersal. '不勝' means 'cannot bear' or 'cannot help feeling,' and '零落' refers to scattering or dispersal.
  • 桃花為風雨所摧 - The peach blossoms were crushed by wind and rain. A poetic expression symbolizing beauty being destroyed by external forces.
  • 拾花瓣砌字 - Picked up petals to form characters. A metaphor for arranging petals to spell out words or phrases, a poetic or artistic act.
  • 春過半，花命也如春短 - Half of spring has passed; the flower's life is as short as spring. A poetic line expressing the fleeting nature of beauty.
  • 一夜落紅吹漸漫 - At night, falling red petals are blown gradually into a spread. A vivid image of petals being scattered by the wind.
  • 風狂春不管 - The wild wind pays no heed to spring. A metaphor for nature's indifference or the inevitability of change.
  • 此真個‘風狂春不管’矣 - This is indeed ‘the wild wind pays no heed to spring’! A direct quote from the text, emphasizing the poetic imagery.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鹽米事煩 - Troubled by salt and rice matters. A euphemistic way of referring to the burdens of daily life and household management, particularly for women in classical Chinese literature.
  • 纨扇 - Silk fan. A common object in classical poetry and painting, often symbolizing beauty, summer, or the passage of time.
  • 賓朋零落 - Scattering of friends. A common theme in classical Chinese literature expressing the sorrow of parting and the passage of time.

Proper Nouns

  • 秋芙 - A name, likely of a woman; should be kept as is.
  • 楊渚白 - A proper name, possibly of a historical figure or a fictional character; should be rendered as 'Yang Zhubai.'
  • 《謁金門》 - A classical ci poetry tune; should be rendered as 'Yejinmen' (《谒金门》), preserving the original title.
  • 錢文涛、費子苕，嚴文樵、焦仲梅 - Names of individuals; should be rendered in transliteration: Qian Wentao, Fei Zishao, Yan Wenqiao, Jiao Zhongmei.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 余旧蓄一绿鹦鹉 - Literally 'I used to keep a green parrot,' where '蓄' means to keep or raise, often in a domestic setting.
  • 字曰“翠娘” - 'Naming it 'Cui Niang,' where '字' means to name, and '翠娘' is a given name for the parrot.
  • 呼之辄应 - 'Calling it would always respond,' where '辄' means always or immediately.
  • 翠娘饮啄常失时 - 'Cui Niang often missed its feeding times,' where '饮啄' refers to drinking and eating.
  • 渐憔悴 - 'Gradually became thin and weak,' where '渐' means gradually, and '憔悴' describes a state of weakness.
  • 一曰 - 'One day,' where '曰' is an archaic form of '日' meaning day.
  • 余起盥沐 - 'I got up to wash,' where '盥沐' means to wash.
  • 恍如秀娟声吻 - 'Seemed like Xiu Juan's voice,' where '恍如' means seems like, and '声吻' refers to voice and manner of speaking.
  • 杨枝去数月矣 - 'Yang Zhi has been gone for several months,' where '去' means to leave or depart.
  • 翠娘有知，亦忆教诗人否 - 'If Cui Niang has any consciousness, does it also remember being taught poetry?' where '有知' means having consciousness, and '忆' means to remember.
  • 秋芙每谓余云 - 'Qiu Fu often tells me,' where '每' means often, and '谓' means to tell.
  • 人生百年，梦寐居半，愁病居半，襁褓垂老之曰又居半 - 'In a hundred years of life, half is spent dreaming, half is spent in sorrow and illness, and half is spent in infancy and old age,' where '梦寐' means dreaming, '愁病' means sorrow and illness, '襁褓' means infancy, and '垂老' means old age.
  • 所仅存者，十之一二耳 - 'Only about one or two tenths remain,' where '仅存' means remaining, and '耳' is a particle emphasizing the small amount.
  • 况我辈蒲柳之质 - 'Especially us, who are as weak as willow and reed,' where '蒲柳' are plants known for their weakness.
  • 犹未必百年者乎 - 'We might not even live to a hundred years,' where '犹' means even, and '未必' means might not.
  • 庾兰成云 - 'Yǔ Lánchéng said,' where '云' means said.
  • 一月欢娱，得四五六曰 - 'One month of joy can be divided into four or five days,' where '欢娱' means joy, and '曰' is an archaic form of '日' meaning day.
  • 想亦自解语耳 - 'It must also be able to understand itself,' where '想' means think or guess, '自解' means to understand oneself, and '耳' is a particle emphasizing the thought.
  • 信然 - 'Indeed so,' where '信' means indeed, and '然' is a particle confirming the statement.
  • 平生未作百里游 - 'I have never traveled a hundred miles in my life,' where '平生' means throughout one's life, and '百里' means a hundred miles.
  • 甲辰娥江之役 - 'The campaign on the E River in the year Jia Chen,' where '甲辰' is a Chinese zodiac year, and '役' means campaign or expedition.
  • 秋芙方病寒疾 - 'Qiu Fu was suffering from a cold,' where '方' means just or just now, and '病' means to be sick.
  • 欲更行期 - 'Wanted to change the travel schedule,' where '更' means to change, and '行期' means travel schedule.
  • 而行装既发 - 'But the travel preparations were already underway,' where '既' means already, and '发' means to start or set out.
  • 黄头促我矣 - 'The boatmen were urging me,' where '黄头' refers to boatmen, and '促' means to urge or hurry.
  • 晚渡钱江 - 'Crossed the Qian River in the evening,' where '晚' means evening, and '渡' means to cross.
  • 飓风大作 - 'A typhoon raged,' where '飓风' means typhoon, and '大作' means to rage or rage fiercely.
  • 隔岸越山，皆低鬟敛眉，郁郁作相对状 - 'On the opposite bank, the mountains seemed to lower their brows and frown, appearing as if they were facing each other,' where '低鬟敛眉' describes a frowning expression, and '郁郁作相对状' means appearing as if they were facing each other.
  • 因忆子安《滕王阁序》云 - 'I recalled what Zǐ Ān wrote in his 'Preface to the Pavilion of Prince Teng,' where '因忆' means to recall, and '云' means said.
  • 天高地迥，觉宇宙之无穷 - 'The sky is high and the earth is far away, making one feel the boundlessness of the universe,' where '迥' means far away, and '觉' means to feel.
  • 兴尽悲来，识盈虚之有数 - 'Joy comes to an end and sorrow follows, recognizing the cyclical nature of life,' where '兴尽' means joy comes to an end, '悲来' means sorrow follows, and '盈虚之有数' means the cyclical nature of life.
  • 殊觉此身茫茫 - 'I felt my life was vague and uncertain,' where '殊觉' means felt, and '茫茫' means vague or uncertain.
  • 不知当置何所 - 'Not knowing where to place myself,' where '不知' means not knowing, and '置' means to place.
  • 明河在天 - 'The bright river is in the sky,' where '明河' refers to the Milky Way.
  • 残灯荧荧 - 'The dim light flickers,' where '残灯' means dim light, and '荧荧' means to flicker.
  • 酒醒已五更时矣 - 'I had woken up from drinking, it was already the fifth watch of the night,' where '酒醒' means to wake up from drinking, and '五更' means the fifth watch of the night.
  • 欲呼添衣 - 'Wanted to call for more clothes,' where '欲呼' means to want to call, and '添衣' means to add clothes.
  • 而罗帐垂垂 - 'But the silk curtains were hanging down,' where '罗帐' means silk curtains, and '垂垂' means hanging down.
  • 四无人应 - 'No one responded,' where '四' means all around, and '无人应' means no one responded.
  • 开眼视之 - 'Opened my eyes to look,' where '开眼' means to open one's eyes, and '视之' means to look at it.
  • 始知此身犹卧舟中也 - 'Then I realized I was still lying in the boat,' where '始知' means then I realized, and '犹卧舟中' means still lying in the boat.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 翠娘 - The name of the parrot, which is personified as having a name and consciousness.
  • 秀娟 - The name of the maidservant who taught the parrot poetry.
  • 杨枝 - A character in the story, possibly a friend or acquaintance.
  • 秋芙 - A person in the story, likely the narrator's wife or close companion.
  • 庾兰成 - A historical figure, possibly a poet or writer.
  • 子安 - Another historical figure, possibly a poet or writer.
  • 滕王阁序 - A famous preface written by Wang Bo, a Tang Dynasty poet.
  • 甲辰 - A Chinese zodiac year, corresponding to a specific period in the lunar calendar.
  • 娥江 - A river, possibly a specific location in the story.
  • 钱江 - The Qian River, a real river in China.
  • 五更 - The fifth watch of the night, a traditional Chinese time division.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 秋月正佳 - A phrase indicating that the autumn moon is at its best, often used to describe a beautiful or serene autumn night.
  • 秋芙命雏鬟负琴 - 秋芙 is a name, likely referring to a person, and 雏鬟 refers to a young servant girl. 负琴 means to carry a zither, a traditional Chinese musical instrument.
  • 放舟两湖荷芰之间 - 放舟 means to set sail or row a boat, 两湖 refers to two lakes, and 荷芰之间 means among lotus and water caltrop plants.
  • 因买瓜皮迹之 - 买瓜皮 is an idiom meaning to follow someone's trail or path, often used metaphorically.
  • 相遇于苏堤第二桥下 - 苏堤 refers to the Su Causeway in Hangzhou, a famous scenic spot, and 第二桥 means the second bridge.
  • 秋芙方鼓琴作《汉宫秋怨》曲 - 鼓琴 means to play the zither, and 《汉宫秋怨》 is the title of a piece of music.
  • 余为披襟而听 - 披襟 means to open one's robe, often used to describe a relaxed and open posture.
  • 斯时四山沉烟，星月在水，琤瑽杂鸣 - 斯时 means at that time, 四山沉烟 describes the mountains shrouded in mist, 星月在水 means stars and moon reflected in the water, and 琛瑽杂鸣 describes the sounds of various objects clinking together.
  • 不知天风声环佩声也 - 天风声 means the sound of the wind, 环佩声 means the sound of jade pendants, and the phrase suggests that the sounds were indistinguishable.
  • 船唇已移近漪园甫岸矣 - 船唇 means the edge of the boat, 漪园甫岸 refers to a specific place, likely a garden or park.
  • 固叩白云庵门 - 固 means to firmly or resolutely, 叩 means to knock, and 白云庵 is the name of a temple or hermitage.
  • 庵尼故相识也 - 庵尼 means a nun, and 故相识 means an old acquaintance.
  • 坐次，采池中新莲，制羹以进 - 坐次 means to sit down, 采池中新莲 means to pick fresh lotus flowers from the pond, and 制羹以进 means to prepare a soup and offer it.
  • 香色清冽，足沁肠睹 - 香色清冽 describes the fragrance and color as fresh and cool, and 足沁肠睹 means it is refreshing to the heart and soul.
  • 其视世味腥膻，何止薰莸之别 - 世味腥膻 refers to the worldly flavors that are unpleasant, and 薰莸之别 means the difference between good and bad smells, suggesting a stark contrast.
  • 回船至段家桥登岸 - 回船 means to return the boat, 段家桥 is the name of a bridge, and 登岸 means to disembark.
  • 施竹簟于地 - 施 means to place, 竹簟 means a bamboo mat, and 于地 means on the ground.
  • 闻城中尘嚣声，如蝇营营，殊聒人耳 - 尘嚣声 means the noise of the city, 如蝇营营 describes the sound as similar to the buzzing of flies, and 殊聒人耳 means it is very disturbing.
  • 桥上石柱，为去年题诗处 - 桥上石柱 refers to the stone pillars on the bridge, and 为去年题诗处 means they were the place where poems were written last year.
  • 近为嫔衣剥蚀，无复字迹 - 嫔衣剥蚀 means the stone has been eroded by the elements, and 无复字迹 means the writing is no longer visible.
  • 欲重书之，苦无中书 - 欲重书之 means to want to write it again, and 苦无中书 means there is no suitable writing material.
  • 其时星斗渐稀，湖气横白 - 星斗渐稀 means the stars are becoming fewer, and 湖气横白 means the lake is covered with a white mist.
  • 听城头更鼓，已沉沉第四通矣 - 更鼓 refers to the drum that signals the time, and 已沉沉第四通矣 means it is the fourth hour of the night.
  • 遂携琴刺船而去 - 携琴 means to carry the zither, 刺船 means to row the boat, and 而去 means to leave.
